hero.title: How RunGoMX handles your information
hero.description: This page explains, in practical terms, what information RunGoMX may receive, why it is used, when it may be shared, and how to reach us with privacy questions.

summary.title: The short version
summary.description: RunGoMX supports race discovery, registrations, payments, results, rankings, and organizer operations. Privacy decisions are shaped by those product flows and by the fact that some event information is provided by organizers.
highlights: We collect the information needed to run accounts, registrations, support conversations, and related event workflows.
We use information to operate the platform, confirm transactions, communicate about events or support, and protect the service.
We may share relevant data with event organizers and service providers involved in payments, communications, hosting, and security.
Questions about privacy, corrections, or account-related concerns can be sent through our contact page.

sections.title: Privacy details for runners, organizers, and visitors
sections.description: These sections are written to stay concrete and readable. They describe the normal ways information moves through the public RunGoMX experience.

informationWeCollect.title: Information we collect
informationWeCollect.intro: The information we receive depends on how you use RunGoMX.
paragraphs: When you browse public pages, we may receive basic technical information needed to render pages safely, keep the service reliable, and understand platform usage at a high level.

When you create an account, register for an event, contact support, or participate in organizer workflows, we may receive profile details, contact information, registration details, payment-related confirmations, and support messages.
bullets: Account and contact details you provide
Registration and participant information submitted for an event
Operational or support messages you send to the team
Basic device, browser, and usage information used for reliability and security

howInformationIsUsed.title: How information is used
howInformationIsUsed.intro: We use information to deliver the product you are actively using.
paragraphs: That includes operating event pages, processing registrations, confirming payments or registration states, showing results and rankings where applicable, and responding to support requests.

We may also use information to improve platform reliability, investigate misuse, prevent fraud, comply with legal or accounting obligations, and keep organizers and participants informed about event-related activity.
bullets: Operate accounts, registrations, and organizer workflows
Send confirmations, updates, and support responses
Protect the platform, users, organizers, and payment flows
Maintain records needed for compliance, reconciliation, and service improvement

whenInformationIsShared.title: When information is shared
whenInformationIsShared.intro: RunGoMX does not treat all information the same way. Sharing depends on the transaction or workflow involved.
paragraphs: Relevant participant or transaction details may be shared with the organizer responsible for the event you interact with, because organizers need information to manage registrations, event logistics, check-in, support, and post-event operations.

We may also rely on service providers that support payments, communications, hosting, analytics, or security. Information may be disclosed when reasonably necessary to comply with law, enforce platform rules, or respond to legitimate legal requests.
bullets: Event organizers for the events they manage
Payment, communications, hosting, and security providers supporting the platform
Authorities or legal processes when disclosure is required or reasonably necessary

eventOrganizerRelationship.title: Event and organizer relationship context
eventOrganizerRelationship.intro: RunGoMX provides the platform, but organizers often control important event-specific decisions.
paragraphs: Event descriptions, pricing, schedules, logistics, race-day instructions, cancellation terms, waivers, and some participant-facing communications may come from the organizer of a specific event.

That means some information you submit through RunGoMX is used to help that organizer operate their event. Organizers may have their own policies, notices, or obligations in addition to what is described on this page.
bullets: Organizers are typically responsible for their event-specific details and decisions
RunGoMX operates the platform layer that powers registrations, discovery, results, rankings, and related workflows

communicationsAndSupport.title: Communications and support
communicationsAndSupport.intro: Some communications are necessary to operate the service you requested.
paragraphs: If you contact us, create an account, register for an event, or participate in an organizer workflow, we may send operational messages related to confirmations, important updates, support replies, or issues affecting the service.
Support conversations may be stored so the team can follow up, understand prior context, and resolve future issues more accurately.
bullets: Registration confirmations and payment-related updates
Support replies and troubleshooting communications
Important notices about event or platform operations

dataRetentionAndAccounts.title: Data retention and account-related considerations
dataRetentionAndAccounts.intro: Retention depends on why the information exists in the first place.
paragraphs: RunGoMX may keep information for as long as it is needed to operate accounts, support registrations, show event participation history, maintain results or rankings, resolve disputes, perform accounting tasks, or protect the platform.

If an account is changed or closed, some records may still need to be retained because they are tied to completed registrations, event operations, public results, legal obligations, or security review.
bullets: Operational records may outlast an individual session or account change
Completed event and transaction history may need to remain available for support, organizer operations, or compliance

userChoicesAndContact.title: User choices and contact for privacy questions
userChoicesAndContact.intro: We want privacy questions to have a clear path.
paragraphs: If you need help with a privacy concern, want to report inaccurate information, or have an account-related question connected to personal data, contact the RunGoMX team through the public contact page.
Requests may require us to verify context and may be limited when records are needed for event administration, completed transactions, public results, security, or legal compliance.
bullets: Use the contact page for privacy and account-related questions
Please include enough detail for us to identify the event, account, or situation involved

cta.title: Questions about privacy or your account?
cta.description: Send the RunGoMX team the details we need to understand the event, account, or transaction involved.

relatedLinks.title: Also review our terms of service
relatedLinks.description: Privacy explains information handling. Our terms explain platform use, organizer responsibilities, and transaction expectations.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.description: Read the rules, responsibilities, and limitations that apply when using RunGoMX.
